cl: Strict Curvature, Uniqueness & Solution Set Structure

units.n: Propositional Logic, Truth-Functional Connectives & Inference Patterns
units.nt: Covers atomic propositions, logical connectives, truth tables, material implication vs causal reasoning, proof methods, and condition formalization; excludes modal/intuitionistic logic, model theory, proof complexity, automated theorem proving, and type theory.

units.n: Quantifier Syntax, Nested Operators & Negation Rules
units.nt: Covers universal/existential quantifiers, domain restriction, nested order sensitivity, De Morgan's laws for quantifiers, negation of complex statements, and natural language translation; excludes quantifier elimination, Presburger arithmetic, higher-order quantification, model semantics, and Skolemization.

units.n: ℝ as Complete Ordered Field & Inequality Axioms
units.nt: Covers field axioms for ℝ, trichotomy/transitivity, absolute value, triangle inequality, interval notation, Archimedean property, density of ℚ, and order preservation; excludes non-Archimedean fields, hyperreals, constructive analysis, measure-theoretic foundations, set-theoretic construction of ℝ, and p-adic systems.

Covers bound definitions, least upper bound property, sup/inf existence, max/min vs sup/inf distinction, attainment conditions, and optimization existence bridge; excludes supremum norm, lattice theory, complete partial orders, order topology, and extended real formalization.

units.n: Set Operations, Cartesian Products & Feasible Region Notation
units.nt: Covers set-builder syntax, subset relations, union/intersection/complement, Cartesian products, orthant definition, and feasible set representation; excludes ZFC axioms, transfinite induction, forcing, large cardinals, category theory, and naive paradox resolutions.

Covers domain/codomain formalism, composition, injectivity/surjectivity/bijectivity, strict/weak monotonicity, inequality preservation, argmax invariance, and ordinal utility foundations; excludes functional analysis spaces, measure-preserving transforms, ergodic theory, recursive function theory, and abstract homomorphisms.

units.n: ℝⁿ Structure, Vector Operations & Euclidean Geometry
units.nt: Covers n-tuple definition, vector operations, orthants, Euclidean inner product, Cauchy-Schwarz, norm derivation, and Pythagorean theorem; excludes infinite-dimensional spaces, Banach/Hilbert theory, weak topologies, dual spaces, and abstract vector space axiomatization.

units.n: Metric Axioms, Distance Functions & Open/Closed Balls
units.nt: Covers metric axioms, norm-induced vs general metrics, open/closed ball definitions, interior/boundary/exterior classification, and neighborhood methodology; excludes metric geometry, Gromov-Hausdorff convergence, Riemannian manifolds, and non-metric topological axiomatization.

units.n: Formal Convergence, ε-N Criterion & Limit Arithmetic
units.nt: Covers sequence definition, ε-N formalism, limit uniqueness, boundedness, limit points, coordinate-wise equivalence, and algebra of limits; excludes net/filter convergence, topological convergence, asymptotic equivalence, and nonstandard analysis.

Covers Cauchy definition/criterion, ℝⁿ completeness, boundedness of convergent sequences, subsequence extraction, Bolzano-Weierstrass preview, and convergence equivalence tests; excludes metric space completion, Baire category theorem, and general topological compactness.

Covers open/closed set definitions, sequential characterization, closure/interior/boundary operators, union/intersection preservation, and nested set intuition; excludes sheaf theory, Čech cohomology, algebraic topology, non-Hausdorff spaces, and separation axioms.

Covers sequential/open-cover compactness, Heine-Borel theorem, compactness preservation, finite subcover property, and infinite-dimensional failure; excludes Tychonoff product compactness, locally compact spaces, Stone-Čech compactification, and proper maps.

units.n: Continuity, Topological Characterization & Extreme Value Theorem
units.nt: Covers ε-δ/sequential continuity, preimage of open sets, compactness preservation, algebra of continuous functions, Extreme Value Theorem, and attainment guarantees; excludes uniform/Lipschitz continuity, weak topology continuity, and topological group continuity.

Covers column/row conventions, matrix operations, transpose properties, matrix classification, dimensional compatibility, and multiplication rules; excludes non-normal matrix spectral theory, Jordan forms, matrix functions, and abstract ring theory.

Covers linear dependence/independence, rank equivalence, elementary operations, full-rank characterization, determinant computation, and invertibility links; excludes exterior algebra determinants, Pfaffians, perturbation bounds, multilinear algebra, and tensor rank.

units.n: Matrix Inversion, Adjoint Construction & Uniqueness Proofs
units.nt: Covers inverse definition, existence/uniqueness proofs, adjoint/cofactor formula, algebraic properties, and rank preservation; excludes generalized inverses, LU/QR/SVD decompositions, iterative algorithms, and operator theory on Banach spaces.

units.n: Single-Variable Foundations: Limits, Continuity & Linear Approximation
units.nt: Covers epsilon-delta limits, continuity, difference-quotient derivative, local linear approximation, and mean value theorem; excludes fractional calculus, distributional derivatives, analytic continuation, and Lebesgue integration.

Covers differentiability via affine approximation, limit formulation, total derivative as linear operator, C¹ class definition, and differentiation rules; excludes Fréchet/Gâteaux differentiation, implicit/inverse function theorem proofs, and Sobolev spaces.

Covers partial/directional derivative computation, gradient vector, total derivative identity, existence vs differentiability counterexamples, and C¹ sufficiency; excludes subgradients, Clarke gradients, Banach space directional derivatives, and Lie derivatives.

units.n: Hessians, Mixed Partials & Second-Order Taylor Expansions
units.nt: Covers second-order partials, Hessian construction, Clairaut's theorem, C² class definition, second-order Taylor expansion, and curvature mapping; excludes higher-order tensors, multivariate Faà di Bruno formula, and Riemannian Hessians.

Covers xᵀAx structure, symmetry, ray invariance, definiteness taxonomy, unit sphere compactness, and principal minor sign rules; excludes indefinite quadratic optimization, semidefinite programming, eigenvalue perturbation, and Clifford algebras.

units.n: Standard Optimization Structure & Solution Set Definitions
units.nt: Covers max/min notation, objective/constraint separation, interior/boundary feasibility, argmax/argmin sets, global optimizers, and non-unique solution handling; excludes multi-objective Pareto fronts, stochastic/robust optimization, and variational inequalities.

Covers parameter space, state-dependent functions, value functions, set-valued policy mappings, and parametric dependence; excludes second-order sensitivity, bifurcation theory, homotopy continuation, and Bayesian optimization.

Covers directional path reduction, FOC derivation via limits, interior optimality necessity, critical point vs optimum distinction, and saddle point geometry; excludes nonsmooth FOCs, variational analysis, and Morse theory.

units.n: FOC Limitations, Non-Differentiable Extensions & Failure Modes
units.nt: Covers necessity vs sufficiency, failure under open/unbounded domains, liminf/limsup framework, kink/corner behavior, and FOC non-optimality proofs; excludes proximal algorithms, bundle methods, and Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman PDEs.

Covers second-order necessary/sufficient conditions, local extrema classification, eigenvalue/minor application, and Taylor justification; excludes saddle point sufficiency, Morse index theory, and degenerate bifurcation analysis.

units.n: Semidefinite Edge Cases, Global Identification & Boundary Procedures
units.nt: Covers zero quadratic forms, inconclusive tests, higher-order expansion necessity, global optimum identification via boundary comparison, and n-dimensional proof architecture; excludes global optimization heuristics, branch-and-bound theory, and NP-hardness proofs.

units.n: Feasible Manifolds, Tangent Spaces & Geometric Tangency
units.nt: Covers equality constraint sets, feasible region topology, tangent space definition, geometric gradient alignment, and multiplier intuition; excludes manifold optimization, Riemannian gradient descent, and implicit function theorem proofs.

units.n: Lagrangian Construction, Critical Systems & Multiplier Existence
units.nt: Covers Lagrangian formulation, DL=0 derivation, first-order necessity, tangency proof, multiplier signs, and solution procedures; excludes augmented Lagrangians, penalty methods, and dynamic programming multipliers.

units.n: Inequality Geometry, Active/Slack Classification & Complementary Slackness
units.nt: Covers inequality constraints, slack/binding zones, active set notation, complementary slackness logic, truth-table derivation, and case enumeration; excludes complementarity theory, variational inequalities, and Farkas' lemma proofs.

Covers formal KKT theorem, non-negativity/slackness, stationarity, max/min sign reversal, constraint qualification dependency, and mixed constraint unification; excludes Fritz John conditions, abnormal multipliers, and nonsmooth KKT.

units.n: Constraint Qualifications, Rank Conditions & Failure Diagnostics
units.nt: Covers LICQ, rank conditions, geometric pathologies, counterexamples for CQ failure, and pre-screening procedures; excludes Mangasarian-Fromovitz/Slater CQs, metric regularity, and infinite-dimensional qualifications.

Covers parametric relaxation, value function definition, chain rule application, shadow price derivation, economic meaning of multipliers, and comparative statics; excludes second-order sensitivity, envelope theorem degeneracy, and LP dual variables.

units.n: Restricted Quadratic Forms & Bordered Hessian Determinants
units.nt: Covers tangent subspace restriction of D²L, necessary vs sufficient curvature, bordered Hessian construction, determinant sign rules, and computational verification; excludes reduced Hessian methods, null-space decomposition, and Schur complements.

Covers Weierstrass application, compactness/continuity requirements, critical point vs optimum distinction, unbounded objective handling, and CQ violation decoupling; excludes coercivity existence, recession cones, and Ekeland variational principle.

Covers convex combination definition, line-segment containment, boundary/interior behavior, preservation properties, and separation hyperplane intuition; excludes convex hull algorithms, polyhedral theory, Krein-Milman theorem, and infinite-dimensional convexity.

Covers epigraph/subgraph definition, convexity via set property, sub-level/super-level geometry, visualization, and equivalence proofs; excludes topological vector space analysis, Fenchel conjugacy, perspective functions, and quasi-convex level sets.

Covers formal Jensen inequality, concave/convex duality, tangent hyperplane support, gradient characterization, and C¹ condition shortcuts; excludes integral/operator Jensen inequalities, conjugate functions, and Fenchel duality.

units.n: Interior Continuity, Directional Derivatives & Almost-Everywhere Differentiability
units.nt: Covers convexity-forced interior continuity, one-sided limits, non-increasing difference quotients, directional derivative existence, negligible kink sets, and optimization relevance; excludes Rademacher's theorem proofs, BV functions, and fractal dimension analysis.

Covers strict inequality definition, affine boundaries, strict Jensen, argmax set convexity, local-to-global equivalence, singleton collapse, and computational determinacy; excludes strong/uniform convexity, Bregman divergences, and log-concavity.

units.n: Global Sufficiency under Convexity & SOC Bypass
units.nt: Covers gradient zero as global optimum guarantee, gradient inequality collapse, second-order check bypass, structural verification workflow, and economic determinacy; excludes convex relaxation bounds, integrality gaps, and DC programming.

Covers quasi-concavity definition, upper contour set convexity, concavity distinction, strict transformation preservation, and regularity conditions; excludes pseudo-convexity, invexity, generalized hierarchies, and revealed preference axioms.

Covers critical point sufficiency under quasi-concavity, boundary behavior, regularity conditions, Cobb-Douglas thresholds, and application limits; excludes quasi-convex programming duality, biconvex optimization, and single-peaked preferences.

Covers value function definition, parameter shift propagation, continuity requirements, value vs solution map distinction, and structural dependency classification; excludes nonsmooth value functions, stochastic value iteration, and robust control functions.

Covers partial derivative equality at optimum, indirect effect cancellation, chain rule proof, smooth interior formalization, and comparative statics shortcut; excludes nonsmooth/stochastic envelope theorems, second-order envelopes, and parametric sensitivity proofs.

units.n: Envelope Extensions: KKT Constraints & Corner Cases
units.nt: Covers KKT extension, binding/slack handling, multiplier contributions, corner adjustments, policy sensitivity, and economic translation; excludes variational inequality envelopes, nonsmooth parametric envelopes, and welfare decomposition.

Covers point-to-set map definition, graph properties, upper/lower hemicontinuity, inverse image characterization, sequential characterization, and compact/convex-valued preservation; excludes measurable correspondences, selection theorems, and random set theory.

units.n: The Maximum Theorem: Continuity of Value & Solution Maps
units.nt: Covers Berge's Maximum Theorem statement, V(theta) continuity, D*(theta) hemicontinuity, convexity refinements, single-valuedness conditions, and sequential compactness proof architecture; excludes metric space parametric optimization, stability analysis, and fixed point theorems.

Covers utility/expenditure duality, profit/cost duality, constraint types, Pareto optimality, welfare function equivalence, and multi-agent aggregation; excludes general equilibrium existence, mechanism design, and behavioral economics deviations.

units.n: Integrated Synthesis: Formulation → Existence → Characterization → Narrative
units.nt: Covers full proof pipeline execution, assumption auditing, necessity vs sufficiency architecture, proof templates, mathematical-to-economic translation, second-best intuition, and two-tier policy design; excludes computational implementation, numerical algorithms, large-scale simulation, and empirical estimation.
